---
title: "Software"
toc: false
---

Open-source tools for conflict and political violence text analysis.

## ConfliBERT GUI

Browser-based interface for running inference and fine-tuning classifiers with [ConfliBERT](https://eventdata.utdallas.edu/conflibert/). Supports named entity recognition, binary and multilabel classification, question answering, and training custom models on user data with seven base architectures. Includes model comparison with ROC-AUC curves.

[GitHub](https://github.com/shreyasmeher/ConfliBERT-gui){target="_blank"}

## conflibertR

R package wrapping ConfliBERT for inference, fine-tuning, and model comparison. Install with `devtools::install_github("shreyasmeher/conflibertR")`.

[GitHub](https://github.com/shreyasmeher/conflibertR){target="_blank"}

---

::: {.callout-tip appearance="minimal" icon=false}
**Citation:** Brandt, P.T., Alsarra, S., D'Orazio, V., Heintze, D., Khan, L., Meher, S., Osorio, J. and Sianan, M., 2025. Extractive versus Generative Language Models for Political Conflict Text Classification. *Political Analysis*, pp.1-29.
:::
